focus: Slice cures, slowing down swing, draw bias driver effectiveness, equipment vs swing debate

PAIN.quote: I went to the driving range about a month ago...sliced every single one of my drives and could not hit my irons solidly for my life. Went out golfing (18 holes) with my family, either sliced or topped every drive. It was horrible
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

PAIN.quote: I spend so much time researching online how to fix a slice. Then spent weeks trying to fix my out-to-in swing path, fix my head being ahead of the ball at impact, trying to turn over my hands more after impact. Nothing. Still was slicing every drive
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix
PAIN.context: Weeks of research and attempts - nothing worked

PAIN.quote: My current driver is a Nike Ignite and it's the only thing in my bag I can't hit. With all my other clubs, it's either middle to middle right. With driver it's in the adjacent fairway
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?

PAIN.quote: I have a problem with slicing also, I took a full swing lesson with cameras at golf tec and they fixed my stance. I'm hitting more consistent but I'm still slicing bad. Some times, the ball will hit the ground and roll laterally to the right!
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

PAIN.quote: My ball usually would turn at about 150 yards to the right hard on most every drive. I worked on many different stances, grips, adjusted the swing, and it always seemed to come back
PAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice

PAIN.quote: Sucks giving up 50 yards to the other guy on every tee shot though
PAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?

HOPE.quote: I just set up over the next ball and took a really nice and slow swing, because I was giving up. And that one went straight down the middle of the driving range, about 180 yards. That was the turning point
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

HOPE.quote: I slowed down my swing and now I'm hitting every shot perfectly straight. I'm so happy but I'm shocked it was something as simple as that!
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

HOPE.quote: I just picked up a new Burner Draw 9.5 stiff and WOW I never thought i'd lose my slice but its gone. My ball flies very straight about 85% of the time now
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice

HOPE.quote: I was fighting a slice for awhile and I got a 10.5 Burner Draw too. I'm actually hitting draws with it and not slicing. Feels much better
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice
HOPE.context: Draw driver turned slice to draw

HOPE.quote: Take a nice, firm, tempoed swing, make good contact and let the club do the work. I still fade the ball about 10 yards but it's very controllable and predictable. I actually like having a natural fade. You always know what it's going to do
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix
HOPE.context: Controlled fade better than erratic slice

HOPE.quote: I'm not surprised... Mine was terrible, then a simple grip change and learning to shift my weight at the right time fixed it
HOPE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Not swinging hard has got to be the number one hardest thing for me to do, as well as most people I see play. Pure contact beats out speed with poor contact every single time
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Not swinging hard is the hardest thing

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: I think what was happening was I was trying to swing so hard that most of my body, including my head, was way in front of the ball at impact--resulting in an open clubface
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: If you have a pull slice, like I used to, it's from swinging out an outside in path. The ball starts left (due to the path of the club coming right to left), and spins back hard right due to the high amount of spin
ROOT_CAUSE.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?

ROOT_CAUSE.quote: Pride gets in the way, and golf ads/golfers make it appear that you should swing hard all the time and its all about distance, but I'd rather hit it straight and at my target everytime, wouldn't you?
ROOT_CAUSE.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix
ROOT_CAUSE.context: Pride and marketing push distance over accuracy

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I have read and watched vids also as well as spent countless $$$ at the range and everytime I thought I had it cured it would come back. That is true 'hide' not cure but as long as its going straight down the fairway or a slight draw sometimes I'm happy
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: It's nice not to have to aim 2 fairways to the right, land on the left side of my fairway just to watch it roll off the right hand side
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: If you're in between clubs, or not sure, CLUB UP and swing soft. It is said so often, but people still don't do it
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix

SOLUTIONS_TRIED.quote: I already have a pretty decent swing. Good enough to hit long irons and my FW's straight, anyway. Driver is my albatross. Guess I'll just leave it at home
SOLUTIONS_TRIED.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: Could the stiff shaft have something to do with it also, or was it just the closed club face?
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: 104mph ss with a reg shaft might not unload fully and your head would be open = slice. A stiff shaft could make a big difference
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice

COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.quote: It can definitely help. But if you don't want to spend the money you can just set the face of your driver closed at address... Again.. This is a temp fix
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?
COMPETITOR_MECHANISM.context: Closed face at address as free fix

VILLAIN.quote: I think, long term, it makes it worse by allowing you to get away with it instead of fixing it
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?

VILLAIN.quote: A club will not fix you. If you want a band aid fix try closing the face of the driver at address
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?

VILLAIN.quote: I know a guy that has his driver set to close and the weights set in a draw bias and it does nothing to stop his slice
VILLAIN.source: GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?
VILLAIN.context: Draw bias settings didn't stop slice

VILLAIN.quote: Swinging slow is simply a bandaid and not a fix. Swinging at the maximum effort that is barely within our control should be our goal
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix
VILLAIN.context: Slowing down is also a bandaid

VILLAIN.quote: The driver may mask your slice, but you've got at least 10 other clubs in the bag you probably want to hit straight
VILLAIN.source: TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice
VILLAIN.context: Draw driver doesn't help other clubs

sources_covered: TheSandTrap - Finally cured my slice...shocking fix
TheSandTrap - Burner draw driver cured my slice
GolfWRX - Can a draw-bias driver fix my slice?
high_value_themes_identified: Draw driver turned slice to 85% straight
Not swinging hard is hardest thing to do
